steps.name: git_commit_report_paths
steps.stdout:
[feature/harden-transfer-file-protocol-rules fd62ea6] Publish remote-next transfer report
 4 files changed, 4405 insertions(+), 796 deletions(-)

forbidden_normal_replacements: direct next_command.py.txt execution by the user
notes: next_command.py.txt is a pure Python payload lane, not the user-facing wrapper command
direct Python execution is reserved for explicit debug or recovery only
required_behavior: read branch from .agentic/transfer/inbox/current.yaml when no branch argument is supplied
block stale, missing, inactive, or head-mismatched transfer orders
synchronize and fast-forward the target branch before local execution
refresh stale rule acknowledgement before transfer execution where possible
run lower-level transfer-order actions through the local transfer runner
print a human summary after report publication

rules_acknowledge_after_commit.trigger: required after every commit because repo_head changes

assistant_after_g: Read the newest remote report or latest pointer from the repository.
Verify whether the report is fresh.
Derive the actual status from evidence, not from the chat signal.
Inspect evidence on FAIL or BLOCK.
Diagnose transfer-uplink problems when the remote report is missing or stale.
Never claim success if the report is stale.
Do not ask for long terminal output while a repo-backed or local diagnosis path is available.

chat_signal.meaning: I ran the standard local command. Read the newest remote-backed transfer report, or, if none is visible, treat the state as BLOCKED or TRANSFER_UPLINK_PROBLEM and decide from available evidence whether the state is PASS, FAIL, BLOCK, or WAIT.

must_fix_first: tests/test_transfer_remote_next.py currently contains a syntax error from the bootstrap script output.
remote-next currently reads branchless orders but is not yet block-tolerant end-to-end.
report publication, commit, rule re-ack, and push must be integrated into one wrapper-owned execution report.
next_implementation_slice.title: Harden remote-next as block-tolerant one-command transfer wrapper

principles: Chat is only the control channel.
Evidence lives in the repository, or, if remote publication is blocked, first in stable local transfer evidence.
The user runs exactly the same local command every time.
All concrete execution steps live in the transfer order or are executed by the remote-next wrapper.
one_command_transfer_protocol.purpose: When direct remote work is not possible because of tool, connector, or safety boundaries, collaboration must use one permanently stable local command. The user must not be required to manually run several transfer, commit, push, diagnostic, pull, or branch commands.

transfer_file_header_requirement.description: This protocol must be stored in a machine-readable form and prepended as a machine-readable header to every local-to-LLM transfer file. The goal is to reduce LLM drift by making the communication contract available inside each transfer artifact.

avoid: inline Python with complex shell quoting
commands that can leave zsh in quote> or dquote>
preferred: Use printf '%s\n' for visible shell output lines
Avoid unterminated single or double quotes
recovery: If quote>, dquote>, or heredoc> appears, press Ctrl-C, then run git status --short and git branch --show-current
